---
title: "SCDMS User Guide"
subtitle: "Submissions & Commission Decision Management System"
subtitle-block-banner: true
author: "Public Service Commission — Vanuatu"
date: last-modified
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
lang: en

format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
    smooth-scroll: true
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: article
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* ── Colour palette matching the app's design language ── */
:root {
  --psc-blue:   #2563eb;
  --psc-indigo: #4f46e5;
  --psc-teal:   #0d9488;
  --psc-amber:  #d97706;
  --psc-red:    #dc2626;
  --psc-green:  #16a34a;
  --psc-slate:  #475569;
}

/* Hero / title block */
#title-block-header {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e3a8a 0%, #2563eb 60%, #0d9488 100%);
  color: white;
  padding: 3rem 2rem 2rem;
  border-radius: 0 0 1rem 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
}
#title-block-header .title    { font-size: 2.2rem; font-weight: 800; }
#title-block-header .subtitle { font-size: 1.1rem; opacity: .85; margin-top: .3rem; }
#title-block-header .author,
#title-block-header .date     { opacity: .75; font-size: .9rem; }

/* Role chips */
.role-chip {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: .2rem .75rem;
  border-radius: 9999px;
  font-size: .78rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  margin: .15rem;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
.role-ministry  { background: #dbeafe; color: #1d4ed8; }
.role-psc       { background: #ede9fe; color: #4f46e5; }
.role-unit      { background: #d1fae5; color: #065f46; }
.role-exec      { background: #fef3c7; color: #92400e; }
.role-compliance{ background: #fce7f3; color: #9d174d; }

/* Stage badges — mirroring the app */
.stage-badge {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: .2rem .65rem;
  border-radius: .4rem;
  font-size: .75rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin: .1rem;
}
.stage-draft       { background: #f1f5f9; color: #475569; }
.stage-submitted   { background: #eff6ff; color: #1d4ed8; }
.stage-received    { background: #f0fdf4; color: #166534; }
.stage-returned    { background: #fff7ed; color: #9a3412; }
.stage-registered  { background: #ede9fe; color: #4c1d95; }
.stage-review      { background: #fefce8; color: #713f12; }
.stage-assessment  { background: #ecfdf5; color: #065f46; }
.stage-commission  { background: #fdf4ff; color: #6b21a8; }
.stage-approved    { background: #dcfce7; color: #14532d; }
.stage-rejected    { background: #fef2f2; color: #7f1d1d; }
.stage-implement   { background: #f0fdfa; color: #0f766e; }

/* Workflow arrows */
.workflow-row {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  align-items: center;
  gap: .4rem;
  margin: .6rem 0;
  font-size: .85rem;
}
.workflow-arrow { color: #94a3b8; font-weight: 300; }

/* Step cards */
.step-card {
  border-left: 4px solid var(--psc-blue);
  background: #f8fafc;
  border-radius: .5rem;
  padding: .9rem 1.1rem;
  margin-bottom: .8rem;
}
.step-card h4 { margin: 0 0 .4rem; color: var(--psc-blue); font-size: .95rem; }
.step-num {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  width: 1.6rem; height: 1.6rem;
  background: var(--psc-blue);
  color: white;
  border-radius: 50%;
  font-size: .75rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin-right: .5rem;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

/* AI feature banner */
.ai-banner {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #4f46e5 0%, #7c3aed 100%);
  color: white;
  border-radius: .75rem;
  padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
  margin: 1rem 0;
}
.ai-banner h4 { margin: 0 0 .3rem; font-size: 1rem; }
.ai-banner p  { margin: 0; opacity: .9; font-size: .88rem; }

/* Tip / info blockquote overrides are handled by Quarto callouts */

/* Table improvements */
table {
  font-size: .88rem;
  width: 100%;
}
thead { background: #f1f5f9; }
th    { font-weight: 600; color: #334155; }
td, th { padding: .5rem .75rem !important; vertical-align: top; }

/* Sidebar TOC fix */
#TOC { font-size: .85rem; }

/* Print */
@media print {
  #TOC { display: none; }
  .callout { break-inside: avoid; }
}
</style>
```

# Introduction {.unnumbered}

The **Submissions & Commission Decision Management System (SCDMS)** is the digital platform of the Public Service Commission (PSC) of Vanuatu. It manages the complete lifecycle of HR submissions — from the moment a ministry prepares a draft through to the commission's decision and the resulting implementation actions.

This guide is written for **all user roles** on the system. Each chapter is clearly labelled with which roles it applies to, so you can focus on the sections relevant to your work.

::: {.callout-tip title="How to read this guide"}
Look for the role labels at the start of each section:

<span class="role-chip role-ministry">Ministry HR / Dept Admin / Head of Agency</span>
<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Officer / Secretary / Commissioner</span>
<span class="role-chip role-unit">Unit Managers & Principals</span>
<span class="role-chip role-exec">OPSC Manager / Senior Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-compliance">Compliance Officer</span>
:::

---

# Signing In & Your Account

## Logging In

Open the SCDMS web address in your browser. You will see the login screen.

1. Enter your **username** and **password**.
2. Click **Sign In**.

::: {.callout-note title="Forgot your password?"}
Click **Forgot password?** on the login screen. Enter your email address and you will receive a reset link. If you do not receive it within a few minutes, check your spam folder or contact your system administrator.
:::

## Two-Factor Authentication

If your account has two-factor authentication (2FA) enabled, after entering your password you will be prompted for a **one-time code**.

- Open your authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator) on your phone.
- Enter the 6-digit code shown for the SCDMS entry.
- The code refreshes every 30 seconds — if it is about to expire, wait for the next one.

## Session Lock & PIN

The system can lock your screen after a period of inactivity, keeping you signed in but requiring a PIN to continue.

- **To set a PIN:** Go to your profile → *Account settings* → *Session PIN*.
- **To lock manually:** Click your avatar in the top-right corner → *Lock session*.
- **To unlock:** Enter your PIN and click *Unlock*.

::: {.callout-warning title="Working on a shared computer?"}
Always lock your session or sign out when you step away from a shared device.
:::

## Profile & Preferences

Click your name or avatar in the top-right corner to access:

| Option | What it does |
|--------|-------------|
| **My Profile** | View your account details and role |
| **Lock session** | Lock the screen until your PIN is entered |
| **Dark / Light mode** | Toggle the colour theme |
| **Language** | Switch between English, French, and Bislama |
| **Sign Out** | End your session completely |

## Installing SCDMS as an App (Optional)

SCDMS works as a **Progressive Web App** — you can install it on your desktop or phone for quick access.

- **Chrome/Edge:** Click the install icon in the browser address bar, then *Install*.
- **iOS Safari:** Tap *Share* → *Add to Home Screen*.
- Once installed, SCDMS opens in its own window without browser chrome.

---

# Navigating the System

## The Main Sidebar

The left-hand sidebar contains your main navigation. The links shown depend on your role — you will only see sections relevant to you.

| Section | Who sees it | Purpose |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| **Dashboard** | Everyone | Overview, metrics, recent activity |
| **Submissions** | Ministry, PSC, Unit Managers | Create, track, and review submissions |
| **Reports** | PSC, Secretariat, OPSC | Generate executive reports |
| **Meetings** | Secretariat, Commissioners | Schedule and manage sittings |
| **Agenda** | Secretariat | Manage commission sitting agendas |
| **Decisions** | Secretariat, OPSC | Record and track commission decisions |
| **Meeting Room** | Secretariat | Recording upload and minutes pipeline |
| **Notifications** | Secretariat | Issue decision notification letters |
| **Tasks** | OPSC Managers | Post-decision implementation tasks |
| **Staff Assistant** | Everyone | AI chatbot for PSC guidance |

## The Header Bar

The top bar gives you quick access to:

- **Search** — search submissions and tasks by reference, title, ministry, or stage
- **Notifications bell** — view unread alerts; badge shows unread count
- **User menu** — profile, lock, theme, sign out

## Notifications

The bell icon in the top-right shows a red badge when you have unread notifications.

- Click the bell to open the notification panel.
- Click a notification to jump to the relevant submission or task.
- Click **Mark all as read** to clear the badge.

You can also enable **browser push notifications** (permission required):

1. Click your profile → *Desktop notification settings*.
2. Click *Enable notifications* and allow the browser prompt.
3. You will now receive alerts even when SCDMS is in a background tab.

---

# Your Role in the System

SCDMS has different roles for different parts of the organisation. Your role determines which actions you can perform and which submissions you can see.

## Role Overview

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Ministry Side

<span class="role-chip role-ministry">Ministry HR Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-ministry">Department Admin Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-ministry">Head of Agency (DG/Director)</span>

Ministry-side users **prepare and submit HR cases** to the PSC. You can:

- Create new submissions and fill in digital forms
- Upload supporting documents
- Run the AI package validation check before submitting
- Track your submission's progress through the PSC workflow
- Respond to requests for clarification (resubmit)
- Use the AI Autofill to check your required documents checklist

You **cannot** advance a submission past the PSC Intake stage or view other ministries' submissions.

### PSC Internal Staff

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Secretary</span>
<span class="role-chip role-psc">Senior Administration Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Commissioner</span>
<span class="role-chip role-psc">Chairperson</span>
<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Administrator</span>

PSC staff **manage the full submission pipeline**. You can:

- Receive, register, and route submissions
- Return submissions for clarification
- Move submissions through all workflow stages
- Annotate and sign documents
- Manage the commission agenda and sittings
- Draft and sign official minutes
- Extract decisions and record outcomes

### Unit Managers & Principals

<span class="role-chip role-unit">VIPAM Manager / Principal</span>
<span class="role-chip role-unit">HR Unit Manager / Principal</span>
<span class="role-chip role-unit">ODU Manager / Principal</span>
<span class="role-chip role-unit">Compliance Manager / Senior / Principal</span>
<span class="role-chip role-unit">CSU Manager</span>

Unit staff **conduct detailed assessments** of submissions routed to their unit. You can:

- View submissions routed to your unit
- Complete the required-document checklist review
- Run AI Autofill on the checklist
- Annotate and sign submission documents
- View and generate quality scores
- Update assessment outcomes

### OPSC Post-Decision

<span class="role-chip role-exec">OPSC Manager</span>
<span class="role-chip role-exec">Principal Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-exec">Senior Officer</span>

Post-decision roles **implement commission decisions**. You can:

- View decisions and implementation tasks assigned to you
- Update task status and add progress notes
- Create sub-tasks and assign them to team members
- Access submission history and documents for context

### Compliance

<span class="role-chip role-compliance">Compliance Manager</span>
<span class="role-chip role-compliance">Compliance Senior Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-compliance">Compliance Principal</span>

Compliance users handle **CMS-linked compliance matters**. You can:

- View submissions routed to the compliance unit
- Manage compliance checklist review
- Access the external Case Management System (CMS) via the portal link
- Annotate and review submission documents

:::

---

# Submissions — Ministry Side

<span class="role-chip role-ministry">Ministry HR Officer · Dept Admin · Head of Agency</span>

This chapter covers everything you need to do from preparing a submission to tracking it through the PSC pipeline.

## Creating a New Submission

1. Click **Submissions** in the sidebar.
2. Click the **New Submission** button in the top-right.
3. Fill in the required fields:

| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | A clear, specific title — e.g. *Appointment of Grade 10 Economist, Department of Finance* |
| **Form category** | Select the relevant HR category (appointment, promotion, discipline, etc.) |
| **Form type** | Select the specific PSC form (e.g. PSC 2-1, PSC 3-7, ORG-3.1) |
| **Ministry** | Your ministry — auto-filled if your account is linked |
| **Department** | The relevant department |
| **Classification** | Sensitivity level of the matter |
| **Notes** | Any background notes for PSC officers (optional) |

4. Click **Save**. A reference number (e.g. `PSC-2026-00123`) is assigned automatically.

::: {.callout-tip title="Good titles save time"}
Avoid vague titles like *"Staff matter"*. Write the person's title, grade, and department. PSC officers process dozens of cases — a clear title helps yours get routed and assessed faster.
:::

## Filling In a Digital Form

For submissions that use a **digitized form** (PSC 2-1, PSC 2-2, PSC 3-7, ORG-3.1, or other form-builder forms), a form panel appears in the submission detail view.

- Fill in all fields marked as required.
- Click **Save form** before navigating away — unsaved changes are lost.
- You can return and edit the form as many times as needed while the submission is in **Draft**.

::: {.callout-note title="PSC Form 2-2 — Live Preview"}
PSC Form 2-2 has a **Preview Form** toggle in the top-right of the form panel. Turn it on to see a formatted preview of how the printed form will look as you fill it in.
:::

## Uploading Supporting Documents

Every submission requires supporting documents. To upload:

1. Open your submission and scroll to the **Documents** panel.
2. Enter an optional **description** (e.g. *Cover letter*, *Medical certificate*).
3. Click **Choose file & upload** and select your file.
4. The file appears in the document list immediately.

**Accepted formats:** PDF, Word (.doc, .docx), Excel (.xls, .xlsx), PNG, JPG, JPEG — maximum 20 MB per file.

::: {.callout-tip title="Describe your documents clearly"}
The description you enter is used by the AI checklist autofill to match documents to the required-document list. Name them precisely — *"Position description — Finance Officer Grade 10"* is far more useful than *"doc1"*.
:::

### Document Processing (OCR)

After upload, the system extracts text from PDFs and images automatically. You will see a status indicator:

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| ⏳ Processing | Text extraction is underway — wait a moment |
| ✅ Done | Text extracted; AI features can now read this document |
| ⚠️ Failed | Extraction could not be completed (scanned image may be low quality) |

OCR extraction runs in the background. The document is available for download immediately; AI features work best once extraction is complete.

## The Required Documents Checklist

The **Required Documents** checklist panel (visible in the submission detail) lists every document your form type requires. Each item shows:

- ☐ or ☑ — whether the document has been confirmed as present
- The document name and description
- Any notes added by you or the AI

### Manually Marking Items

Click the checkbox next to any item to toggle it between present (☑) and missing (☐). You can also expand an item with the chevron (∨) to add a note explaining the status.

### AI Autofill — Automatic Suggestions {#sec-autofill}

<div class="ai-banner">
<h4>✦ AI Feature — Checklist Autofill</h4>
<p>Upload your documents first, then let the AI analyse them and suggest which checklist items are satisfied. You review and confirm each suggestion — the AI never changes the checklist without your approval.</p>
</div>

**To use AI Autofill:**

1. Upload all your supporting documents and wait for OCR to complete.
2. In the **Required Documents** panel, click **AI Autofill**.
3. The system analyses all extracted document text — this takes a few seconds.
4. Each checklist item receives a colour-coded suggestion:
   - 🟢 **AI suggests: present** — the AI found evidence in your uploaded documents
   - 🟡 **AI suggests: missing** — no matching evidence found
   - A short note explains what the AI found (or didn't find)
5. Review each suggestion:
   - Click **Accept** to confirm a single item and save the AI's reasoning as a note
   - Click **Reject** to dismiss a suggestion and leave the item unchanged
   - Click **Accept all suggestions** to confirm all items at once

::: {.callout-warning title="Always review AI suggestions"}
AI Autofill is a time-saving assistant, not an automatic approver. The system may occasionally suggest an item is present based on partial text matches. Review each note before accepting — especially for critical documents.
:::

## Validating Your Package Before Submission {#sec-validate}

Before you submit to PSC, run the **AI Package Validation** check. This reviews your entire draft package and flags potential issues.

<div class="ai-banner">
<h4>✦ AI Feature — Package Validation</h4>
<p>The AI checks your required documents checklist, uploaded files, and form data for gaps that could cause the PSC to return your submission for clarification.</p>
</div>

1. Open your submission (must be in **Draft** stage).
2. Scroll to the **Validate package before submit** panel at the top.
3. Click **Validate package**.
4. The AI returns a summary and a list of issues grouped by severity:

| Severity | Meaning | Action |
|----------|---------|--------|
| 🔴 **Critical** | Will likely block processing | Must fix before submitting |
| 🟡 **Warning** | Should fix if possible | Recommended before submitting |
| ℹ️ **Info** | Nice-to-have improvements | Optional |

5. Fix any critical issues, then run validation again.
6. When ready, use the **Move to Next Stage** panel → select **Submitted to PSC** → click **Apply Transition**.

::: {.callout-note title="Submitting with critical gaps"}
If critical gaps remain, the system will warn you when you attempt to submit. You can choose *Submit anyway* to proceed despite the gaps — but the PSC may return the submission for clarification. Use this option only when you have a good reason.
:::

## Tracking Your Submission

Once submitted, you can monitor progress on the **Submissions** list and in the submission detail.

### Submission List

- Go to **Submissions** in the sidebar.
- Your submissions appear with their current stage, reference number, and last-updated date.
- Use the search bar to filter by reference, title, or stage.

### Submission Detail — Progress Bar

Open any submission to see:

- **Stage badge** — current stage in colour-coded format
- **Progress bar** — visual indication of where the submission sits in the lifecycle
- **Timeline** — full history of every stage transition, who made it, when, and any remarks

### Returned for Clarification

If the PSC returns your submission, you will receive a notification. The stage will show:

<span class="stage-badge stage-returned">Returned for Clarification</span>

1. Open the submission and read the PSC remarks in the timeline.
2. Make the required changes (upload missing documents, edit form data, etc.).
3. In **Move to Next Stage**, select **Resubmitted** and add a brief note explaining what you changed.
4. Click **Apply Transition**.

---

# Submissions — PSC Staff

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Officer · Secretary · Senior Admin Officer</span>

## The Submissions Log

The **Submissions** list is your main workspace. It shows all submissions visible to your role.

- **Search:** Use the search bar to find by reference (e.g. `PSC-2026-00123`), title, ministry, or stage.
- **Filter by stage:** Click the stage filter chips to narrow the list.
- **Quality column:** The AI quality score (0–100) is shown to help triage effort required.
- **Overdue flag:** Submissions exceeding the 21-day assessment target are marked overdue.

## Receiving and Registering Submissions

When a ministry submits a case, it arrives at **Submitted to PSC**. As a PSC Officer:

<div class="workflow-row">
<span class="stage-badge stage-submitted">Submitted to PSC</span>
<span class="workflow-arrow">→</span>
<span class="stage-badge stage-received">Received by PSC</span>
<span class="workflow-arrow">→</span>
<span class="stage-badge stage-registered">Registered & Routed</span>
</div>

1. Open the submission and review the documents and form data.
2. In **Move to Next Stage**, select **Received by PSC** and add any intake remarks.
3. After formal registration, transition to **Registered and Routed** — select the appropriate unit in the routed unit field.

### Returning for Clarification

If the submission is incomplete:

1. Select **Returned for Clarification** in **Move to Next Stage**.
2. Enter clear remarks explaining exactly what is missing or incorrect.
3. The ministry user receives a notification and can resubmit.

::: {.callout-tip title="Write clear return remarks"}
Vague remarks like *"missing documents"* create confusion and delays. Be specific: *"Position description for Grade 10 Finance Officer is missing — please attach the current approved PD"*.
:::

## Setting Assessment Deadlines

The PSC targets a **21-day assessment turnaround**. To set the deadline:

1. Open the submission detail → scroll to the **Quick Info** panel (right column).
2. The **Assessment deadline** field shows the current deadline if set.
3. Overdue submissions are flagged with a red *Overdue* tag throughout the system.

## Moving Through Workflow Stages

Use the **Move to Next Stage** panel (right column of submission detail) to advance or move a submission. Only stages permitted for your role and the current stage are shown.

::: {.callout-note title="Stage transition remarks"}
Adding remarks when you transition a stage creates a permanent audit record. PSC Officers should always add remarks when returning for clarification, routing, or deferring — this protects both the PSC and the ministry.
:::

## Annotating Documents

PSC Officers and Secretariat can **annotate PDF documents** to add comments, highlight sections, or draw attention to issues.

1. In the **Documents** panel, click **Annotate** next to a PDF.
2. The annotation editor opens. Use the toolbar to:
   - Draw freehand with the pen tool
   - Add text annotations
   - Highlight areas with shapes
3. Click **Save annotations** when done.
4. Other authorised users can see your annotations — they are overlaid on the document.

## Signing Documents

Authorised users (Secretariat, Commissioners, OPSC Managers) can place a digital signature on PDF documents.

1. In the **Documents** panel, click **Sign** next to a PDF.
2. The signature editor shows the document. Choose your signature placement.
3. If you have a saved signature in your profile, it is used automatically; otherwise, draw one.
4. Click **Place signature** and confirm the position.
5. A timestamp and your name are recorded with the signature.

## AI Features for PSC Officers

### AI Executive Brief {#sec-brief}

<div class="ai-banner">
<h4>✦ AI Feature — Executive Brief</h4>
<p>The AI reads all uploaded documents and generates a concise executive summary of the submission — what is being asked, the background, and flags for attention.</p>
</div>

The brief appears automatically for submissions visible to Secretariat staff. To regenerate:

- Open the submission → scroll to the **AI executive brief** panel.
- Click **Regenerate** if the brief is outdated or shows an error.
- The brief takes 10–30 seconds to generate.

### AI Quality Score {#sec-quality}

<div class="ai-banner">
<h4>✦ AI Feature — Quality Score</h4>
<p>The AI scores the submission on Completeness, Clarity, Evidence Quality, and PSC Formatting. Higher scores mean less rework is likely needed.</p>
</div>

The quality score (0–100) appears in the submission list and detail. To re-score:

- Open the submission → scroll to the **Submission quality score** panel.
- Click **Re-score**.

**Interpreting the score:**

| Score range | Effort estimate |
|-------------|----------------|
| 75–100 | Low review effort — generally well-prepared |
| 50–74 | Moderate review effort — some gaps likely |
| 0–49 | High review effort — significant gaps expected |

### Deadline Reminder Drafts {#sec-deadlines}

When a submission has an assessment deadline set, the system can generate **AI-drafted reminder emails** for ministry contacts and assigned officers. These are draft templates only — no email is sent automatically.

- Scroll to the **AI deadline reminder drafts** panel.
- Personalised drafts are shown for each contact type.
- Copy the draft into your email client and send manually.

---

# Submissions — Unit Managers & Assessment

<span class="role-chip role-unit">VIPAM · HR Unit · ODU · Compliance · CSU Managers & Principals</span>

Submissions routed to your unit appear in your **Submissions** list with the stage:

<span class="stage-badge stage-review">Manager Checklist Review</span> or <span class="stage-badge stage-assessment">Under Assessment</span>

## Checklist Review Stage

At **Manager Checklist Review**, your primary task is to verify that all required documents are present.

1. Open the submission → go to the **Required Documents** panel.
2. For each item, confirm whether the document is present:
   - Check the checkbox if the document is uploaded and complete.
   - Add a note if needed (e.g. *"Not applicable — temporary appointment"*).
3. Use **AI Autofill** to speed up this review (see @sec-autofill).
4. Once all items are confirmed, transition to **Under Assessment**.

::: {.callout-warning title="Checklist blocking"}
If required documents are not confirmed as present, the system may block transition to later stages. Ensure all items are properly marked or have a recorded explanation.
:::

## Conducting Assessment

At **Under Assessment**, your unit conducts its detailed review according to PSC procedures.

Use the submission detail to access:

- **Documents** — read all uploaded supporting files; annotate as needed
- **AI Executive Brief** — quick summary generated from the document content
- **Quality Score** — triage indicator for expected effort
- **Workflow Timeline** — full history of the case

When assessment is complete, transition to the appropriate next stage based on your unit's outcome.

## ODU Restructure Checklist

For organisational restructure submissions (form type **ORG-3.1**), an additional **ODU Restructure Checklist** form appears. ODU Officers fill in all 20 checklist items, then submit the checklist. The ODU Manager then approves it.

---

# Commission Sittings & Agenda

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Secretary · Senior Admin Officer · PSC Commissioner · Chairperson</span>

## Scheduling a Sitting

1. Go to **Meetings** in the sidebar.
2. Click **New Sitting**.
3. Fill in the details:

| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | e.g. *Ordinary Sitting No. 12 — 2026* |
| **Type** | Ordinary, Special, Flying Minute, or Emergency |
| **Date & Time** | Scheduled sitting date and start time |
| **Venue** | Meeting room or virtual |
| **Notes** | Any additional context |

4. Click **Save**. The sitting status starts as **Scheduled**.

## Building the Agenda

1. Go to **Agenda** in the sidebar.
2. Select the sitting you want to build the agenda for.
3. Click **Add agenda item**.
4. Select a submission from the list — only submissions in **Forwarded to Commission** are shown.
5. Optionally set a sequence number to control the order of discussion.
6. Repeat for all items.

**Agenda status flow:**

<div class="workflow-row">
<span class="stage-badge stage-draft">Draft</span>
<span class="workflow-arrow">→</span>
<span class="stage-badge stage-submitted">With Secretary for Approval</span>
<span class="workflow-arrow">→</span>
<span class="stage-badge stage-received">Secretary Approved</span>
<span class="workflow-arrow">→</span>
<span class="stage-badge stage-commission">Circulated to Members</span>
</div>

::: {.callout-note title="Removing agenda items"}
Items can be removed from the agenda before it is finalised. Once circulated, changes require a new agenda version to be created.
:::

## AI Sitting Briefing Pack

<div class="ai-banner">
<h4>✦ AI Feature — Sitting Briefing Pack</h4>
<p>Before the sitting, generate an AI-written overview of the agenda — ready items, deferred matters, overdue cases, and quality score distribution.</p>
</div>

1. Open the sitting from the **Meetings** list.
2. Click **Generate briefing pack**.
3. The AI compiles the briefing from all agenda items — takes 10–20 seconds.
4. Use it to prepare for the chair's opening remarks or to brief commissioners.

---

# Meeting Room & Minutes Pipeline

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Secretary · Chairperson</span>

Commission sittings are recorded to ensure an accurate permanent record. SCDMS manages the full pipeline from recording to signed minutes.

## Recording Requirements

::: {.callout-important title="Required: Logitech GROUP Conference System"}
Official commission sittings must use the **Logitech GROUP** push-to-talk conference system, not laptop or phone microphones. The system is calibrated to produce clean audio without echo or overlap — essential for accurate transcription and the Bislama AI repair step.

Contact your IT support if the conference system is not available or not working.
:::

A **setup guide** for the Logitech GROUP is accessible inside the **Meeting Room** section of SCDMS. It walks you through connecting the hub to Zoom or Microsoft Teams and testing the microphone.

## The Minutes Pipeline

The minutes workflow follows five steps:

::: {.step-card}
<span class="step-num">1</span> **Upload the Recording**

In **Meeting Room**, select the sitting and drag-drop (or browse for) the Zoom or Teams export file. Maximum size: 500 MB. Once uploaded, the system is ready for transcription.
:::

::: {.step-card}
<span class="step-num">2</span> **Paste or Generate a Transcript**

You have two options:

- **Paste from Zoom:** After the meeting, download the Zoom auto-transcript (`.vtt` or plain text) and paste it into the transcript panel.
- **AI Transcribe:** Click *AI Transcribe* to run automatic speech recognition on the uploaded recording. Wait for processing to complete.

::: {.callout-warning title="Bislama in automatic transcripts"}
Zoom and Teams ASR does not recognise Bislama. Bislama words are often transcribed as garbled English phonetics (e.g. *"sore eye"* instead of *"save"*). You **must** repair the transcript before generating minutes.
:::
:::

::: {.step-card}
<span class="step-num">3</span> **Repair Bislama Text (Claude)**

1. Click **Copy Claude prompt** — a pre-filled prompt containing the raw transcript is copied to your clipboard.
2. Open [claude.ai](https://claude.ai) in your browser, paste the prompt, and send.
3. Claude returns a corrected, professional-English transcript.
4. Copy Claude's output back into the SCDMS transcript panel and click **Save transcript**.
:::

::: {.step-card}
<span class="step-num">4</span> **Generate Draft Minutes**

Click **Generate minutes**. The AI drafts formal minutes from the repaired transcript — including agenda items discussed, key points raised, and decisions made.

Review and edit the draft in the minutes editor. The editor supports rich text formatting.
:::

::: {.step-card}
<span class="step-num">5</span> **Extract Decisions & Chair Sign-Off**

1. Click **Extract decisions** — the AI identifies decision outcomes from the minutes text.
2. Review each extracted decision and confirm or edit.
3. Once the minutes are finalised, the **Chairperson** opens the minutes and clicks **Sign minutes**.
4. The signature is placed with a timestamp, completing the formal record.
:::

---

# Decisions & Post-Decision Workflow

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Secretary · Senior Admin Officer</span>
<span class="role-chip role-exec">OPSC Manager · Principal Officer · Senior Officer</span>

## Recording a Decision

After the commission sitting is completed:

1. Go to **Decisions** in the sidebar.
2. Find the submission and click **Record decision**.
3. Select the outcome: **Approved**, **Rejected**, **Deferred**, or **Returned**.
4. Add any remarks or conditions from the commission discussion.
5. Click **Save decision**.

The submission transitions to the appropriate post-decision stage automatically.

## Issuing Decision Notifications

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Secretary</span>

After a decision is recorded, the affected ministry must be notified.

1. Go to **Notifications** in the sidebar.
2. Find the notification record for the decision.
3. Review and edit the notification letter template as needed.
4. Select the notification channel: **Letter**, **Email**, or **Portal notice**.
5. Click **Mark as issued** when the letter has been sent.
6. Once the ministry confirms receipt, click **Mark as acknowledged**.

## Implementation Tasks

<span class="role-chip role-exec">OPSC Manager · Principal Officer · Senior Officer</span>

After a decision is entered and assigned, implementation tasks appear in your **Tasks** list.

### Viewing Your Tasks

1. Go to **Tasks** in the sidebar (or the **Tasks** section on the dashboard).
2. Filter by status: **Open**, **In Progress**, or **Completed**.
3. Click a task to open it.

### Working with Tasks

| Action | How |
|--------|-----|
| **Start a task** | Open the task → click *Mark In Progress* |
| **Add an update** | Open the task → type in the *Add update* field → click *Save update* |
| **Create a sub-task** | Open the task → click *Add sub-task* → fill in details |
| **Assign to someone** | Open the task → click the *Assignee* field → select a user |
| **Complete a task** | Open the task → click *Mark Completed* |
| **Set a due date** | Open the task → click the *Due date* field |

::: {.callout-tip title="Use updates to keep a record"}
Add brief progress updates whenever you take a significant action on a task. This creates a searchable audit trail and keeps managers informed without requiring separate emails.
:::

---

# AI Features Reference

SCDMS includes several AI-powered features to save time and improve submission quality. All AI features require an internet connection. They augment — but do not replace — human review and judgment.

## Overview of AI Features

| Feature | Who can use it | Where to find it |
|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Checklist Autofill** | Ministry HR, PSC Officers, Unit Managers | Submission detail → Required Documents panel |
| **Package Validation** | Ministry HR, PSC Officers | Submission detail → Validate package panel (Draft stage only) |
| **Executive Brief** | PSC Secretary, Senior Admin, PSC Admin | Submission detail → AI executive brief panel |
| **Quality Score** | PSC Officers, Unit Managers, OPSC Managers | Submission detail and submission list |
| **Deadline Reminder Drafts** | PSC Secretary, Officers | Submission detail → Deadline drafts panel |
| **Sitting Briefing Pack** | PSC Secretary, Chairperson | Meeting detail → Generate briefing pack |
| **AI Transcribe** | PSC Secretary | Meeting Room → Transcription step |
| **AI Minutes Draft** | PSC Secretary | Meeting Room → Minutes step |
| **Decision Extraction** | PSC Secretary | Meeting Room → Decisions step |
| **Staff Assistant** | Everyone | Sidebar → Staff Assistant |
| **AI Reports** | PSC Officers, Secretariat, OPSC | Reports page |

## Checklist Autofill

See @sec-autofill for the full step-by-step guide.

**How it works:** The AI reads the extracted text from all uploaded documents and matches it against the named required-document items. It gives each item a present/missing suggestion with a one-sentence explanation of its reasoning.

**Best results:** Upload and allow OCR to complete for all documents before running Autofill. Use descriptive document descriptions when uploading.

## Package Validation

See @sec-validate for the full guide.

**How it works:** The AI reviews the entire draft package — checklist status, uploaded files, form fields, endorsements, and title — and produces a gap report with severity levels.

**Limitation:** Validation only runs on drafts. Once submitted, rerun validation by returning the submission to draft (PSC Officer action).

## Executive Brief {#sec-brief-detail}

**How it works:** The AI reads all uploaded document text and the submission metadata, then writes a 3–5 paragraph executive brief covering: the request, background, key documents, and any flags for the secretariat.

**When to use:** Use it to quickly understand an unfamiliar submission, or to prepare briefing notes before a sitting.

**Regenerate when:** New documents are uploaded, or the brief seems outdated.

## Quality Score {#sec-quality-detail}

**How it works:** Four dimensions are scored by the AI:

| Dimension | What it measures |
|-----------|----------------|
| **Completeness** | Are all expected sections and documents present? |
| **Clarity** | Is the writing clear and professionally worded? |
| **Evidence Quality** | Are supporting documents appropriate and sufficient? |
| **PSC Formatting** | Does the submission follow PSC structure and format? |

The four scores are averaged into an overall score (0–100). The score is recalculated each time you click *Re-score*.

## Staff Assistant (AI Chatbot) {#sec-chatbot}

<div class="ai-banner">
<h4>✦ Staff Assistant</h4>
<p>Ask the Staff Assistant anything about PSC procedures, which form to use, case status, or policy guidance — in English or Bislama.</p>
</div>

1. Click **Staff Assistant** in the sidebar.
2. Type your question in the chat box and press **Enter** or click **Send**.
3. The assistant responds immediately.

**What you can ask:**

- *"Which form do I use for a temporary acting appointment?"*
- *"What is the status of PSC-2026-00042?"* — paste a reference number for an instant case summary
- *"What documents are needed for a promotion submission?"*
- *"Wanem samting mi mas putum long PSC 3-7?"* — Bislama questions are supported

::: {.callout-warning title="Not legal advice"}
Staff Assistant guidance is based on PSC procedures and documentation. It is not a substitute for formal legal advice or official PSC rulings. For critical decisions, always confirm with your unit manager or the relevant PSC officer.
:::

## AI Reports

<span class="role-chip role-psc">PSC Officers · Secretariat · OPSC Managers</span>

The **Reports** page lets you generate executive and operational reports using plain-English prompts.

1. Go to **Reports** in the sidebar.
2. Describe what you want in the text box — e.g.:
   - *"Show all approved appointment submissions from the Ministry of Finance in 2026"*
   - *"List overdue ODU decisions with implementation status"*
   - *"Summary of commission decisions by outcome for Q1 2026"*
3. Set any additional filters (date range, unit, status).
4. Click **Generate report**.
5. Review the report preview and download as **CSV**, **PDF**, or **HTML**.

---

# Dashboard & Reports

<span class="role-chip role-psc">All PSC users · OPSC Managers</span>

## Dashboard Overview

The **Dashboard** is your landing page after sign-in. It shows real-time metrics relevant to your role.

### Key Metrics Cards

| Card | What it shows |
|------|--------------|
| **Total submissions** | All submissions in the system (your visible scope) |
| **Completion rate** | Percentage of received submissions that have been decided |
| **Active cases** | Submissions currently in the active processing pipeline |
| **Overdue** | Submissions exceeding the 21-day assessment target |

### Submissions Activity Chart

The bar chart shows **submissions received vs. decided** over your selected time range. Use the day / week / month selector to zoom in or out.

### Recent Submissions

A live list of the most recently updated submissions — click any row to open the detail view.

### Recent Activity Feed

Shows the latest system events:

- New submission logged
- Stage advanced
- Decision recorded
- Notification issued
- Deadline passed
- Agenda finalised

### Workflow Performance Radar

A six-dimension radar chart showing system performance across: Processing, Compliance, Timeliness, Completion, Quality, and Volume.

## Submission List — Advanced Filtering

From the **Submissions** page:

- **Search bar:** Filter by reference, title, ministry, or stage text.
- **Stage filter:** Click stage chips to show only submissions in that stage.
- **Ministry filter:** Narrow by ministry or department.
- Results paginate at 15 per page; use the navigation at the bottom to move between pages.

---

# Account Security & Best Practices

## Strong Passwords

- Use a password of at least 12 characters with a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols.
- Do not share your password with colleagues — each user must have their own login.
- Change your password if you suspect it may have been compromised.

## Two-Factor Authentication

Enable 2FA on your account for an extra layer of protection:

1. Go to your profile → **Security settings**.
2. Follow the on-screen steps to link an authenticator app.
3. Save your backup codes in a secure place.

## Session Management

- **Lock your screen** whenever you step away — even briefly.
- **Sign out completely** at the end of each working day, especially on shared devices.
- **Do not leave the system unlocked** on an unattended device with sensitive submissions visible.

## Reporting Issues

If you notice unexpected behaviour, data that looks incorrect, or think your account may have been accessed without your knowledge:

1. Sign out immediately.
2. Contact your unit manager or PSC IT support.
3. Use the **Feedback** button (flag icon in the sidebar or bottom of any page) to report bugs or system errors — attach a screenshot if possible.

---

# Quick Reference

## Workflow Stage Reference

| Stage | Who acts | Next steps |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| <span class="stage-badge stage-draft">Draft</span> | Ministry HR | Fill form, upload docs, validate, submit |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-submitted">Submitted to PSC</span> | PSC Officer | Receive and acknowledge |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-received">Received by PSC</span> | PSC Officer | Register and route |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-returned">Returned for Clarification</span> | Ministry HR | Fix and resubmit |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-registered">Registered & Routed</span> | Unit Manager | Begin checklist review |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-review">Manager Checklist Review</span> | Unit Manager | Confirm required documents |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-assessment">Under Assessment</span> | Unit Principal | Conduct detailed assessment |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-commission">Forwarded to Commission</span> | Secretariat | Add to agenda |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-commission">Commission Sitting</span> | Commissioner / Chair | Deliberate and decide |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-approved">Approved</span> | Secretariat | Record decision, issue notification |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-rejected">Rejected</span> | Secretariat | Record decision, notify ministry |
| <span class="stage-badge stage-implement">Under Implementation</span> | OPSC Manager | Execute and report implementation |

## Common Actions — Quick Links

| I want to… | Go to… |
|------------|--------|
| Create a new submission | Submissions → New Submission |
| Upload a document | Submission detail → Documents panel |
| Run AI checklist autofill | Submission detail → Required Documents → AI Autofill |
| Validate before submitting | Submission detail → Validate package panel |
| Check a submission status | Submissions (search by reference) |
| Ask about PSC procedures | Staff Assistant |
| View my tasks | Tasks (sidebar) |
| Generate a report | Reports (sidebar) |
| Manage sitting agenda | Agenda (sidebar) |
| Upload a meeting recording | Meeting Room → Select sitting |
| Lock my screen | User menu (top right) → Lock session |

## Document Checklist — What to Prepare Before Submitting

Before submitting a case to PSC, ensure you have:

- [ ] A clear, specific submission title (not generic)
- [ ] The correct form type selected and all fields completed
- [ ] All required documents uploaded (see your checklist panel)
- [ ] AI Autofill run and suggestions reviewed
- [ ] AI Package Validation run with no critical issues (or a documented reason for proceeding)
- [ ] DG / Head of Agency endorsement recorded where required
- [ ] Document descriptions filled in clearly

---

# Getting Help

## Staff Assistant

The fastest way to get an answer is the **Staff Assistant** — available from the sidebar at any time. Ask in plain English or Bislama.

## Contact PSC Support

For issues the Staff Assistant cannot resolve:

- Speak to your **unit manager** or assigned PSC officer.
- Use the **Feedback** button in SCDMS to report a bug or request a feature.

## System Status

If the system is unavailable or showing an error, try:

1. Refreshing the page (press F5 or Ctrl+R).
2. Clearing your browser cache and signing in again.
3. Checking your internet connection.
4. Contacting your unit manager if the issue persists.

::: {.callout-note title="Working offline"}
SCDMS works in your browser. If your internet connection drops, an **Offline** banner appears at the top of the screen. Recently-visited pages may still load from cache, but you cannot submit forms or upload documents until the connection is restored.
:::

---

::: {.callout-note title="Document version"}
This guide reflects SCDMS version 1.0. Features and workflows may be updated — the Staff Assistant can answer questions about the latest system behaviour.
:::
